Hiring teams

How to hire a video editor

The fastest way to hire a strong editor is to write a brief that filters for the actual workflow, not just taste.

Define the editing job

Name the format, target length, source footage volume, weekly output, deadline pattern, review process, and where the editor fits in the production workflow.

Share references and constraints

Editors need examples of pacing, caption style, graphics level, music direction, and brand boundaries. A good reference saves rounds of revision.

Screen for process

Portfolio quality matters, but process predicts whether the working relationship survives. Ask how they receive notes, organize assets, handle deadlines, and communicate risk.

Need an editor?

Send a hiring brief and start the early matching queue.
